options.label: GitHub-backed static controlled path first
options.description: Use the current static/GitHub/Vercel architecture with governed generated artifacts, no real backend/Auth activation at first.
advantages: Allows controlled article/public index workflow before database activation.
risks: Not ideal for multi-user concurrent editorial operations.
Real Admin/Editor actions still need carefully controlled implementation.

options.label: Supabase/Auth-backed implementation now
options.description: Activate backend/Auth/database for Admin/Editor workflow immediately.
advantages: Better for true multi-user login and database-backed queue state.
Cleaner long-term model for action audit and role controls.
risks: Potential recurring cost and maintenance overhead.
Premature because current public flow has not yet been live-tested.

options.label: Hybrid staged path
options.description: Proceed first with GitHub-backed static controlled go-live, then move Supabase/Auth/backend later when the editorial-publication workflow is stable and real multi-user/database needs are unavoidable.
advantages: Balances safety, cost and implementation speed.
Defers backend/Auth until it becomes necessary.
Creates a clean future upgrade path.
risks: Requires discipline to not bypass the later Supabase/Auth reminder checkpoint.
Some Admin/Editor actions may remain simulated or controlled until later activation.
